RESEARCH ARTICLE

The making of “good” citizens: China's Social
Credit Systems and infrastructures of social
quantiﬁcation

Fan Liang1                    |   Yuchen Chen2

1
 Division of Social Sciences, Duke Kunshan
University, Kunshan, China                         Abstract
2
 Department of Communication and Media,            This article examines citizen scoring in China's Social
University of Michigan, Ann Arbor, USA             Credit Systems (SCSs). Focusing on 50 municipal
                                                   cases that potentially cover 210 million population,
Correspondence                                     we analyze how state actors quantify social and
Fan Liang, Division of Social Sciences, Duke       economic life into measurable and comparable
Kunshan University, Innovation Bldg 3038,
No. 8 Duke Ave, Kunshan, 215316 Jiangsu,           metrics and discuss the implications of SCSs through
China.                                             the lens of social quantiﬁcation. Our results illustrate
Email: fan.liang@duke.edu                          that the SCSs are envisioned and designed as social
                                                   quantiﬁcation practices including two facets: a nor-
                                                   mative apparatus encouraging “good” citizens and
                                                   social morality, and a regulative apparatus disciplin-
                                                   ing “deviant” behaviors and enforcing social manage-
                                                   ment. We argue that the SCSs illustrate the signiﬁ-
                                                   cant shift in which state actors increasingly become
                                                   data processors whereas citizens are reconﬁgured as
                                                   dataﬁed subjects that can be measured, compared,
                                                   and governed. We suggest that the SCSs function as
                                                   infrastructures of social quantiﬁcation for enforcing
                                                   social management, constructing differences, and
                                                   nudging people towards desired behaviors deﬁned by
                                                   the state.

KEYWORDS
                                                   China, credit scores, data, infrastructures, Social Credit Systems,
                                                   social quantiﬁcation
